uk: Преподобних отців наших Симеона, Христа ради юродивого, та Івана, співпосника його
saints.en: Ven. Simeon, Fool-for-Christ, & John his Companion. St. Prophet Ezekiel

rubrics:
Вечірня: катизма чергова. На «Господи, взиваю» 6 стихир: 3 прпп., 3 прор., Сл., І нині: богород. Прокімен дня. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прпп., Сл.: прор., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прор. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. прпп. і прор.
Утреня: по «Бог Господь»: тропар прпп. (2), Сл.: прор., І нині: богород. відпустовий за гласом тропаря прор. Катизми чергові, сідальні Октоїха. Канон Октоїха, прпп. і прор. На 9-ій пісні – «Чеснішу від херувимів». По 9-ій – світильний Октоїха, прпп., Сл.: прор., І нині: богород. Стиховня Октоїха. По «Отче наш»: тропар прпп., Сл.: прор., І нині: богород. за гласом тропаря прор. і за днем тижня. Відпуст середній з помин. прпп. і прор.
Часи: на 1-му – тропар дня; на 6-му – храму; на 3-му – прпп. та 9-му – прор.
Літургія: вся Служба – дня. Ап. – 1 Кор. 152 зач.; 12, 12-26. Єв. – Мт. 76 зач.; 18, 18-22; 19, 1-2; 13-15.

troparion_en.text: Having heard the voice of Your Apostle Paul: / we are fools for Christ’s sake! / Your servant Simeon, O Christ God, / lived the life of a fool here on earth for Your sake. / Therefore as we venerate his memory, / we entreat You, O Lord, to save our souls!

kontakion_en.text: Let us praise with fervent love, / this man who lived in the flesh as an angel, / adorning his soul with the most radiant virtues! / Simeon, the equal to the Apostles and the Bearer of God. / Together with him, let us honor his companion John, / for they both ever stand before God, interceding for us all!

matched_oca_saint: Venerable Simeon of Emessa the Fool-For-Christ, and his fellow ascetic Venerable John

troparion.text: We celebrate the memory / of Your prophet Ezekiel, O Lord; / through him we entreat You, / save our souls.

kontakion.text: (Podoben: “Today You have shown forth...”)Enlightened by the Spirit, your pure heart became the dwelling place of most splendid prophecy; / for you saw things far off as if they were near.  / Therefore, we honor you, blessed and glorious Prophet Ezekiel.

oca_saints.name: Venerable Simeon of Emessa the Fool-For-Christ, and his fellow ascetic Venerable John

troparion.text: Having heard the voice of Your Apostle Paul: / we are fools for Christ’s sake! / Your servant Simeon, O Christ God, / lived the life of a fool here on earth for Your sake. / Therefore as we venerate his memory, / we entreat You, O Lord, to save our souls!

kontakion.text: Let us praise with fervent love, / this man who lived in the flesh as an angel, / adorning his soul with the most radiant virtues! / Simeon, the equal to the Apostles and the Bearer of God. / Together with him, let us honor his companion John, / for they both ever stand before God, interceding for us all!

oca_saints.name: Venerable Onuphrius the Silent and Venerable Onesimos the Recluse, of the Kiev Caves

troparion.text: By a flood of tears you made the desert fertile, / and your longing for God brought forth fruits in abundance. / By the radiance of miracles you illumined the whole universe! / O our holy father Onuphrius, pray to Christ our God to save our souls!

oca_saints.name:
The Finding of the honored relics of the Venerable right-believing Great Princess and Nun
Anna of Kashin, Wonderworker

troparion.text: Having been enlightened by Divine grace, O Venerable one,   /  and having bound your wise soul to the love of Christ, with uprightness of mind, you scorned temporal, corruptible, and earthly goods.   /  You armed yourself courageously against noetic enemies with the Sign of the Cross,   /  extinguishing the coals of the passions by the ascetical struggles of fasting and prayer.   /  O Anna worthy of glory,   /  even after death you pour forth grace upon those who come to your relics.  /  Now, in the heavenly Bridal Chamber with the wise virgins,1 pray to Christ for us who honor your holy memory.

kontakion.text: Devoutly let us sing to Saint Anna,  / who is ready to hear us quickly, the helper of all who are in trouble,  / gathering today with love on the day of the recovery of her revered relics.  / Let us sing a song to the One God in Trinity with rejoicing, /  for He has accounted us worthy to behold the most pure treasure of her revered relics, / which were hidden for many years, and then many and various healings took place and were revealed to us.  / By her prayers to God, she delivers us from all the troubles which befall us.  / With joyful souls and triumphant hearts, we sing hymns of thanksgiving saying,  / "Rejoice, O bulwark of our city."
